title: Inventing with Machines: Generative AI and the Evolving Landscape of IS Research

payload.title: Inventing with Machines: Generative AI and the Evolving Landscape of IS Research
payload.authors: Gopal, R. D., Li, J., Riemer, K., Sarker, S., Singh, P. V., Susarla, A., Bichler, M., & Thatcher, J. B.
research_questions: How is generative AI reshaping the research processes in information systems (IS)?
What are the risks and opportunities associated with the integration of AI in scholarly research?
How can the IS community develop frameworks for responsible AI use in research?
key_findings: Generative AI can automate significant portions of the research process, including hypothesis generation and literature synthesis.
The integration of AI in research poses risks to scholarly integrity, including issues of authorship and the propagation of biases.
A three-level usage model for AI in research (copy editor, research assistant, super-collaborator) is proposed to guide responsible AI integration.
theoretical_contributions: The concept of 'automation of invention' is introduced, highlighting the capabilities of AI in enhancing research productivity.
A framework for responsible AI use in research is developed, emphasizing transparency, provenance, and verification.
The editorial discusses the implications of AI for the identity and methodological diversity of IS research.
limitations_acknowledged: The authors acknowledge the potential for AI to undermine scholarly integrity and the need for human oversight in research processes.
limitations_unacknowledged: The paper does not address the potential for AI to exacerbate inequalities in research capabilities among institutions and researchers.
It overlooks the long-term implications of AI on the labor market for researchers, particularly in terms of job displacement.
follow_up_questions: What specific measures can be implemented to ensure the integrity of AI-assisted research?
How can the IS community balance innovation with the preservation of scholarly values?
What future research is needed to explore the long-term impacts of AI on academic publishing and peer review processes?
payload.apa_citation: Gopal, R. D., Li, J., Riemer, K., Sarker, S., Singh, P. V., Susarla, A., Bichler, M., & Thatcher, J. B. (2025). Inventing with machines: Generative AI and the evolving landscape of IS research. *Information Systems Research, 36*(4), 1949-1967. https://doi.org/10.1287/isre.2025.editorial.v36.n4
